⚠️ **Why This Needs Human Review**
This issue requires human review because it involves a reported major security vulnerability in Claude, which requires immediate attention and verification by a security expert.

📋 **What To Do Next**
To ensure the security of our users, please contact our security team directly via email at [security@claude.com](mailto:security@claude.com) and provide a detailed description of the vulnerability, including steps to reproduce it. Alternatively, you can also submit a report through our website's "Contact Us" form, selecting "Security Issue" as the subject.

📝 **Information To Have Ready**
When reaching out to our security team, please have the following information ready:
- A clear description of the security vulnerability
- Steps to reproduce the issue
- Any relevant screenshots or logs
- Your contact information for follow-up

🔗 **Useful Links**
Unfortunately, we don't have specific documentation available for this issue. However, our security team will guide you through the process and provide any necessary information.

⏱️ **What To Expect**
Once you've reported the issue, our security team will review and verify the vulnerability as soon as possible. We will then work to resolve the issue and provide a fix, which may involve releasing a patch or update. You can expect to receive updates on the status of the issue and any necessary next steps from our security team.
justification: The user reported a security vulnerability, which requires immediate admin attention and escalation.

issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
subject: Website Data crawl
response:
👋 I understand that you want Claude to stop crawling your website, and I'm here to help you with the process.

✅ **Solution / Steps**
To block Claude from crawling your website, you can follow these steps:
1. Check if your website has a `robots.txt` file, which is a standard way to communicate with web crawlers.
2. If you have a `robots.txt` file, add the following line to block Anthropic's crawler: `User-agent: AnthropicDisallow: /`
3. If you don't have a `robots.txt` file, you can create one and add the above line.
4. Alternatively, you can also use the `meta` tag in your website's HTML header to block Anthropic's crawler: `<meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow">`
5. For more information on blocking Anthropic's crawler, you can refer to the article [Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler)

🔗 **Relevant Links**
- [Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler)
- [Claude is providing incorrect or misleading responses. What’s going on?](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8525154-claude-is-providing-incorrect-or-misleading-responses-what-s-going-on)

⚡ **Next Steps / Fallback**
If the above steps don't work or you need further assistance, please contact Anthropic's support team with the following information:
- Your website's URL
- The steps you've taken so far to block the crawler
- Any error messages or issues you're experiencing
You can reach out to support at [support@anthropic.com](mailto:support@anthropic.com) or visit the support page for more contact options. Have this information ready to ensure a smooth and efficient resolution process.
justification: This is a website crawl opt-out request, which can be addressed with a reply.

issue: I need urgent cash but don't have any right now & only the VISA card
subject: Urgent need for cash
response:
👋 I understand that you're in need of urgent cash and only have a Visa card, and I'm here to help you find a solution.

✅ **Solution / Steps**
To get urgent cash with your Visa card, you can try the following steps:
1. Look for an ATM that accepts Visa cards. You can use the Visa ATM locator tool on the Visa website to find one near you.
2. Insert your Visa card into the ATM and enter your PIN to access your account.
3. Select the "Cash Withdrawal" option and follow the prompts to enter the amount you need.
4. Take your cash and remember to take your card and receipt.

If you're having trouble finding an ATM or need further assistance, you can also try:
1. Visiting a bank that accepts Visa cards and speaking with a teller about withdrawing cash.
2. Using a Visa card cash advance service, if available, by calling the phone number on the back of your card.

🔗 **Relevant Links**
For more information on using your Visa card for cash withdrawals, you can visit the [Visa Consumer Support](https://www.visa.co.in/support.html) page.

⚡ **Next Steps / Fallback**
If you're unable to get cash using the above steps, you can contact Visa support for further assistance. You can call Visa at 000-800-100-1219 or [send an email](https://usa.visa.com/Forms/contact-us-form.html) to report any issues. Please have your card details and a description of the problem ready when you contact support.
justification: The customer is seeking guidance on accessing emergency cash, which is a common support topic for Visa.

issue: I am allowing Claude to use my data to improve the models, how long will the data be used for?
subject: Personal Data Use
response:
👋 We appreciate your interest in understanding how your data is used to improve Claude's models and the duration of your data usage for model improvement.

✅ **Solution / Steps**
To find out more about how your data is used for model training, follow these steps:
1. Check your account settings to see if you have opted-in to allow Claude to use your chats and coding sessions for model training.
2. Review the **[Is my data used for model](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/)** training article to understand how Claude uses data from consumer products for model improvement.
3. If you have specific questions about your data usage, refer to the **[How up-to-date is Claude's training data?](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8114494-how-up-to-date-is-claude-s-training-data)** article to learn more about Claude's knowledge cutoff and model updates.
4. Explore the **[model selector](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8664678-how-can-i-change-the-model-version-that-i-m-chatting-with)** to choose a model that best fits your needs and understand the differences between models.

🔗 **Relevant Links**
- [Is my data used for model](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/)
- [How up-to-date is Claude's training data?](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8114494-how-up-to-date-is-claude-s-training-data)
- [Model selector](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8664678-how-can-i-change-the-model-version-that-i-m-chatting-with)

⚡ **Next Steps / Fallback**
If you have followed these steps and still have questions about your data usage or model improvement, please contact our support team at [support@claude.com](mailto:support@claude.com) with your account information and a detailed description of your inquiry. Have your account details and any relevant conversation history ready to ensure a prompt and accurate response.
justification: The user is asking a how-to question with steps likely found in the documentation regarding data retention.

issue: one of my employee has left. I want to remove them from our hackerrank hiring account
subject: Employee leaving the company
response:
👋 I understand you're trying to remove a former employee from your company's HackerRank hiring account, and I'm here to guide you through the process.

✅ **Solution / Steps**
To remove a former employee from your company's HackerRank hiring account, follow these steps:
1. Log in to your **HackerRank for Work** account using your credentials, ensuring you have Company Admin permissions.
2. Click on **Settings**.
3. Access the **Candidates** Page in HackerRank or the relevant page where you manage users or employees.
4. Search to find the former employee you want to remove.
5. Click on the delete option or a similar action that allows you to remove the user from your account.

🔗 **Relevant Links**
For further assistance or to understand more about managing candidates and users, you can refer to the HackerRank documentation or contact [support@hackerrank.com](mailto:support@hackerrank.com) for specific queries.

⚡ **Next Steps / Fallback**

Have your company's HackerRank account details and the former employee's information ready to facilitate a smoother resolution. Additionally, ensure you have a clear description of the issue you're facing to help the support team address your concern efficiently.
justification: The issue is a straightforward account management request that can be addressed with standard procedures.

issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
subject: Claude for students
response:
👋 I understand that your professor wants to set up the Claude LTI key for students, and I'm here to guide you through the process.

✅ **Solution / Steps**
To set up the Claude LTI key, follow these steps:
1. Review the essential resources for getting started with Claude for Education, specifically the guide for Admins, Owners, and Primary Owners: [Getting Started with Claude for Education at Your University (for Owners/Admins)](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/).
2. Ensure you have a Claude for Education account and have generated a developer key in Canvas.
3. In Canvas, go to Admin -> Settings -> Apps.
4. Click "View App Configurations" then select "+ App."
5. Select **Configuration Type** “By Client ID.”
6. Input the Client ID generated for your developer key.
7. Click "Install" and refresh the course page.

🔗 **Relevant Links**
For more information, you can refer to the [FAQs for student, faculty, and other users](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11139144-faqs-on-using-claude-for-education-at-your-university).

⚡ **Next Steps / Fallback**
If you encounter any issues during the setup process or have questions about your Claude for Education plan account, contact your university’s administrator(s) for assistance. If you're unable to resolve the issue with their help, you can reach out to Claude support for further guidance. Please have your account details and a description of the issue ready when contacting support.
justification: The issue is a straightforward LTI setup question that can be addressed with documentation and troubleshooting steps.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend
response:
👋 I understand that you're being asked to spend a minimum of $10 on your Visa card by a merchant in the US Virgin Islands, which is causing some inconvenience for you.

✅ **Solution / Steps**
1. **Check the type of Visa card you're using**: If you're using a Visa credit card, the merchant is allowed to set a minimum transaction amount of $10 in the US Virgin Islands, according to Visa's rules.
2. **Verify the merchant's policy**: If you're using a Visa debit card, the merchant should not be setting a minimum transaction amount. You can politely ask the merchant to clarify their policy.
3. **Notify your Visa card issuer**: If the merchant is refusing to accept your Visa card due to a minimum or maximum amount requirement that doesn't align with Visa's rules, you should notify your Visa card issuer.

🔗 **Relevant Links**
- [Visa Travel Services | Visa Travel Credit Card Support](https://www.visa.co.in/support/consumer/travel-support.html)
- [Fill out this form](/Forms/visa-rules.html) if you have concerns involving a merchant.

⚡ **Next Steps / Fallback**
If the steps above don't resolve the issue, you can contact your Visa card issuer for further assistance. Make sure to have your card details and the merchant's information ready when you reach out. If you're not sure who to contact, you can visit the Visa website to find the contact information for your card issuer. Additionally, you can fill out the form on the Visa website to report any concerns you have about the merchant.
justification: The user is asking for clarification on a minimum spend rule, which is a common issue that can be addressed with a reply.
